summary: Jeff Atwood shares a web comic from Bug Bash that satirizes how software developers mistakenly believe they represent typical users. The comic illustrates the common disconnect between developers and the actual end users of their software. Atwood endorses the comic for hitting 'close to home' and links to related strips exploring how this misconception leads good software astray.
key_points: Software developers falsely assume they are representative of typical users
This self-perception blindspot contributes to software quality problems
The Bug Bash comic effectively captures this industry-wide issue
Developer assumptions about users can cause good software to go bad
money_quotes: Software developers truly believe, in their heart of hearts, that they are typical users.
key_insight: Developers' false belief that they represent typical users is a fundamental cause of software usability failures.
